title: UPS Battery Monitoring
spl:
index=power sourcetype="snmp:ups"
| where battery_status!="normal" OR runtime_remaining_min < 30 OR battery_temp_c > 35
| table _time, ups_name, battery_status, charge_pct, runtime_remaining_min, battery_temp_c
description: UPS battery failure during power loss causes complete outage. Proactive monitoring prevents unprotected power events.
value: UPS battery failure during power loss causes complete outage. Proactive monitoring prevents unprotected power events.
implementation: Poll UPS via SNMP every 5 minutes. Alert on low charge (<80%), low runtime (<30 min), high temperature (>35°C), or abnormal status. Track battery health trend to predict replacement needs.
visualization: Gauge (charge %), Line chart (runtime trend), Table (UPS status), Single value (runtime remaining).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: SNMP TA (UPS-MIB).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: SNMP UPS-MIB (upsEstimatedMinutesRemaining, upsBatteryStatus, upsBatteryTemperature).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Poll UPS via SNMP every 5 minutes. Alert on low charge (<80%), low runtime (<30 min), high temperature (>35°C), or abnormal status. Track battery health trend to predict replacement needs.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=power sourcetype="snmp:ups"
| where battery_status!="normal" OR runtime_remaining_min < 30 OR battery_temp_c > 35
| table _time, ups_name, battery_status, charge_pct, runtime_remaining_min, battery_temp_c
```

Understanding this SPL

**UPS Battery Monitoring** — UPS battery failure during power loss causes complete outage. Proactive monitoring prevents unprotected power events.

Documented **Data sources**: SNMP UPS-MIB (upsEstimatedMinutesRemaining, upsBatteryStatus, upsBatteryTemperature). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): SNMP TA (UPS-MIB). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: power; **sourcetype**: snmp:ups. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=power, sourcetype="snmp:ups". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where battery_status!="normal" OR runtime_remaining_min < 30 OR battery_temp_c > 35` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **UPS Battery Monitoring**): table _time, ups_name, battery_status, charge_pct, runtime_remaining_min, battery_temp_c


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the PDU UI or SNMP poll for the same devices, readings, and time range.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Gauge (charge %), Line chart (runtime trend), Table (UPS status), Single value (runtime remaining).
grandmaExplanation: We watch the physical data center for power, cooling, environment, and physical access.
knownFalsePositives: Power spikes during equipment power cycling, scheduled load shifting, or DR drills.
